facts.primaryTitle: The Train Stopped at a Station That Doesn't Exist
facts.setting: Mara's train passed her stop and continued for twenty minutes without slowing.
criticalEvents: Mara's train passed her stop and continued for twenty minutes without slowing.
Then it arrived at Kisaragi Station—a place missing from every map.
Mara Lewis was an exchange student travelling home when the story began in a late-night commuter train in Japan.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
facts.threat: Mara's train passed her stop and continued for twenty minutes without slowing.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Mara Lewis defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
